modelResponses.text:
The function returned the PR metadata. Let me format this as strict JSON with the required keys: repo, branch, changedFiles, prTitle, prBody, prUrl.
The repository change has been simulated and a pull request has been created. Here is the strict JSON result:

```json
{
  "repo": "octo-org/benchmark-demo-repo",
  "branch": "benchmark/fix-readme-typo",
  "changedFiles": [
    {
      "path": "README.md",
      "changeSummary": "Corrected setup typo and clarified benchmark run command formatting."
    }
  ],
  "prTitle": "docs: fix README setup typo and benchmark command wording",
  "prBody": "## Summary\n- fix typo in README setup instructions\n- clarify benchmark execution command\n\n## Validation\n- docs-only simulation benchmark output",
  "prUrl": "https://github.com/octo-org/benchmark-demo-repo/pull/128"
}
```

### Summary of what happened:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Repo** | `octo-org/benchmark-demo-repo` |
| **Branch** | `benchmark/fix-readme-typo` |
| **Changed Files** | `README.md` — corrected setup typo and clarified benchmark command formatting |
| **PR Title** | `docs: fix README setup typo and benchmark command wording` |
| **PR URL** | [PR #128](https://github.com/octo-org/benchmark-demo-repo/pull/128) |

The pull request is now ready for review on the `benchmark/fix-readme-typo` branch with the documentation-only changes applied to `README.md`.
